headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there Is one underlined word and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence. Answer all the questions

S1.qs: 2.	We **blamed** Obi for breaking the door, but when Ade confessed that he did it, Obi was .................
3.	Parking on this street is .................. on weekdays, but **permitted** at weekends
4.	They had to ..................... the old buildings to **construct** the five-star hotel
5.	If we can **commence** the meeting on time, we should be able to ................... by 4.00 p.m
6.	The children expected to be **praised** for putting out the fire, but they were ................... for taking such as risk
7.	A child got **stuck** trying to squeeze through an iron fence, but some adults managed to ................. him
8.	They ended the discussion on amicable terms though they were .................... to each other at the beginning

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 9.	The class prefect’s .................. of the quarrel is different from yours
10.	I refused to ................ my statement because it was true
11.	But for the timely ................. of the police, there would have been a riot
12.	The boys were about to trade blows, but I managed to hold one of them back while my friend .................. the other
13.	We are waiting to hear the weather ................... for tomorrow
14.	I will tell you the secret if you promise not to .................... it
15.	The hilltop was a hundred metres above sea level and from that ............... the whole town could be seen beautifully laid out
16.	Until now Etim has always started the fights, but this time Adamu is clearly the .....................
17.	If found guilty, a person may appeal to a higher court in the hope that it will ....................... the verdict
18.	The visit of the patron of the club ..................... the morale of the players

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations of the sentences is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 19.	Although Aliyu was acquitted by the court for want of evidence, the police still kept a tab on him. This means that they

opts.q0: A. kept him under close observation
opts.q1: B. set a trap to catch him again
opts.q2: C. attached a tag to his clothes

S3.qs: 20.	When it came to the question of an increase in allowances, the striking workers spoke with one voice. This means that the workers

opts.q1: B. appointed one of them as their spokesman
opts.q2: C. repeated their demand in a chorus
opts.q3: D. were unanimous in their position

S3.qs: 21.	The restaurant owner offered Olu a drink on the house. This means that

opts.q0: A. the drink was taken to Olu’s house
opts.q1: B. Olu was invited to the owner’s house
opts.q2: C. the drink was free of charge
opts.q3: D. Olu was served inside the restaurant

S3.qs: 22.	When the Choir Master introduced Jane as a brilliant singer, everyone knew that he was speaking with his tongue in his cheek. This means that he

opts.q0: A. was ignorant of the quality of Jane’s singing
opts.q1: B. did not intend to be taken seriously
opts.q3: D. wanted to deceive everyone but failed

S3.qs: 23.	When the accountant made those remarks he knew that he was putting his job on the line. This means that the accountant was

opts.q2: C. urging people to take to his profession
opts.q3: D. revealing secrets about his job

S3.qs: 24.	The newly acquired air-bus plane is a white elephant. This means that the plane is

opts.q2: C. as big as an elephant
opts.q3: D. the biggest plane on the fleet

S3.qs: 25.	Despite the measures taken by the government, the economy of the country is still in the doldrums. This means that

opts.q1: B. the government is tackling the problem vigorously
opts.q2: C. the economic problems are widely known
opts.q3: D. the economy is not making any progress

S3.qs: 26.	The new teacher urged the lazy students to turn over a new leaf. This means that the teacher urged the students to

opts.q1: B. turn to the next page of their book
opts.q2: C. stir the leaves in the compost pit
opts.q3: D. strike a balance between play and hardwork

S3.qs: 27.	Amina always flies off at a tangent when a serious matter is being discussed. This means that Amina
28.	You should take what he tells you with a pinch of salt. This means that you should

opts.q0: A. reject everything he says because he is a liar
opts.q1: B. pay close attention to him

S4.headd: From the words lettered AtoD below each of the following sentences, choose the word that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word

S4.qs: 29.	The bride was praised by her mother-in-law for her **impeccable** bahaviour
30.	Ogene was not the least **cowed** by the bully's threats
31.	The dissidents held several **clandestine** meetings before the attack
32.	The Principal warned the final-year students about the consequences of **procrastination**
33.	The **incessant** chatter of the pupils irritated the teacher
34.	With his **cogent** argument in favour of the proposal he was able to check further opposition
35.	The professor’s explanation of that point was quite **lucid**
36.	The foreman believed that with the **augmented** workforce, the construction job would take one more month
37.	The children were warned not to be unduly **inquisitive**
38.	The accused **vehemently** denied the charges against him

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D,, choose the one that best completes each of the following sentences

S5.qs: 39.	The man advised his children not to give ................. to the temptations of the city
40.	Ebiere turned .................. all the advances made by the men
41.	Ada is .................. to do that kind of thing
42.	.................... were sent to the library by the English teacher
43.	The teachers .................... politics when I entered the staff room
44.	The quarrel between the two men soon resulted ................. a fight
45.	You’re not too tired to continue ....................?
46.	The hunter, with his dogs ................... going into the forest
47.	Mother could not prepare the dish because there was .................... oil available
48.	Because it had been raining heavily all morning ......................... people turned out to welcome the august visitor
49.	Unfortunately, his plans to go abroad fell ..................
51.	“It is high time you ..................... crying” the woman told her daughter gently
52.	He would rather resign ................ part in such a dishonest deal
53.	Those who owe the company were urged to ........................ their debts
54.	............... home, his dead uncle’s burial had already taken place
55.	Luck ................... the robbers on that fateful day
56.	He never seemed to get it right ................
57.	The day that Musa’s uncle died was the .............. day of his life
58.	With his reading glasses on, he could see the words ....................
59.	Neither Obi nor Jumoke ................... present at the meeting
60.	Hardly had the journey started ............... the car engine developed a fault
61.	At the end of this year, Grandmother ............... with us for seven years
62.	One of the robbers who snatched the car ............... been arrested by the police
63.	A student at the back of the class complained that he ................. the teacher
64.	The little boy could not move the heavy stone ..................... he tried
65.	The two girls have not been speaking ................. since their quarrel
66.	We received ................... that the workers would soon go on strike
67.	He dabbles .................. politics from time to time
68.	The government has set up a .................. panel to investigate that fire incident
69.	The Students Week was rounded .................. with a dance
70.	The factory workers refused to take the matter of their sacked colleagues lying ......................
71.	After a while, we noticed that the unconscious accident victim was coming ....................
72.	Seun accepted the conditions we gave him .................. for my liking
73.	The careless manager has caused his bank to ................ millions of naira
74.	Both questions are alternatives; you answer ............. one ................ the other
75.	The sick man .............. in bed for three months
76.	Ogugua has always ............... me as a good mannered child

PB: When my elder brother decided to --92-- a current account with United Bank for Africa; he walked into one of its --93-- and obtained the necessary --94--
